options.b: It provides continuity of policy and is more efficient.

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Exp) Option c is the correct answer.**

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Exp) Option d is the correct answer.**

1. Dissolution of the State Legislative Assembly
2. Removal of the Council of Ministers in the State
3. Dissolution of the local bodies

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Out of the following statements, choose the one that brings out the principle underlying the Cabinet form of Government:
questions.questionText: Out of the following statements, choose the one that brings out the principle underlying the Cabinet form of Government:

options.a: An arrangement for minimizing the criticism against the Government whose responsibilities are complex and hard to carry out to the satisfaction of all.
options.b: A mechanism for speeding up the activities of the Government whose responsibilities are increasing day by day.
options.c: A mechanism of parliamentary democracy for ensuring collective responsibility of the Government to the people.
options.d: A device for strengthening the hands of the head of the Government whose hold over the people is in a state of declin

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Exp) Option c is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a feature of Indian federalism?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a feature of Indian federalism?

options.a: There is an independent judiciary in India.
options.b: Powers have been clearly divided between the Centre and the States.
options.c: The federating units have been given unequal representation in the Rajya Sabha.
options.d: It is the result of an agreement among the federating units.

questions.explanationMarkdown: **Exp) Option d is the correct answer.**
